text.title: AI Overview Risk Scoring Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every AI Overview Risk Scoring application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: Score each query on six axes (intent_class, panel_persistence, snippet_length, monetisation, citation_eligibility, alternative_value). Missing an axis = REJECT.
Risk index maps deterministically to skip / target / hedge. Custom overrides require a documented rationale.
rule.rationale: Per-query intuition does not scale; deterministic mapping does.

rule.statement: Re-score every 90 days OR on a material AIO panel change in the niche.
rule.rationale: AIO behaviour drifts; sunk-cost retention is the failure mode.

rule.statement: Monetisation axis uses analytics or revenue model — not gut estimate. Source path required.
rule.rationale: Monetisation by intuition manufactures decisions to keep writing what feels important.

rule.statement: Every decision row carries a one-line rationale and named owner. Anonymous decisions = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Without a named owner, the next quarter re-litigates everything.

rule.statement: Decision record carries semver + last_reviewed. Re-baseline quarterly.
rule.rationale: Stale records lead to stale strategy.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
